items.reason: site/index.html and site/docs/index.html render as HTML with navigation, styling, and links to OpenAPI pages rendered via Redoc (e.g., openapi/oneroster-core.html, hosted-json.html). Deterministic check confirms no .md/.yaml/.yml hrefs anywhere under site/.

site/index.html contains explicit 'Platform Provides' and 'App Developer Owns' sections naming OneRoster, CASE, QTI, Caliper, LTI, Open Badges/CLR, plus app-owned items like 'pedagogy', 'UI quality', and 'scope minimization' / 'data minimization'.

Each generator (e.g., scripts/generate_oneroster_core.py, generate_qti_core.py, generate_case_core.py, generate_caliper_core.py, generate_integration_governance_core.py) reads dictionary/*.v1.json and emits SQL comments, OpenAPI JSON, Markdown, and HTML in one run. scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py verifies SQL/OpenAPI/Markdown/HTML descriptions match the dictionary source so hand-edits would fail the check.

docs/lead-spec-accounting.md and the coverage matrix list all seven Lead specs (OneRoster, QTI, CASE, Caliper, LTI, Security Framework, Data Privacy) with corresponding dictionary files. integration-governance-core.v1.json covers LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy with many objects (lti_registration, lti_deployment, lti_launch, security_oauth_client, privacy_data_sharing_rule, etc.), not placeholders.

Each Lead spec has unsupported/deferred ledgers with reasons referencing scope or downstream impact (e.g., 'QTI delivery/runtime: Repository/search/import/export is separate from assessment delivery conformance'), but coverage is incomplete: docs/lead-spec-accounting.md acknowledges OneRoster full 1.2 (demographics, agents, resources, score scales, AR Profile) is only documented in the broader dictionary and 'not yet executable'. Backlog item 10 explicitly tracks expanding to full OneRoster 1.2 — partial coverage with backlog is `partial`, not `pass`, per the rubric.

scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py enforces every object/field/value is generated. Sampled descriptions are plain-language: e.g., person.display_name → 'The name shown in user interfaces.', organization → 'A district, school, department, program, or other education organization.', class.title → 'The class name people see.' These read as layperson explanations, not jargon.

demo/server.js + demo/schema.sql + demo/test.js implement a runnable OneRoster slice. demo/test.js explicitly asserts `/users/USER-ADA` returns OneRoster-shaped JSON (`user.sourcedId === 'USER-ADA'`, no internal `id` exposed) and that `/users/person_ada` returns 404 — confirming spec-shaped responses. VERIFY.md runs `cd demo && npm run reset-db && npm test`.

scripts/check_supabase_rest.py hits the live https://qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.supabase.co PostgREST URL with the publishable key and verifies `/people`, `/class_roster`, and `/gradebook_results` return 200s. docs/supabase-hosted-database.md confirms verification on 2026-04-26 and runtime prereqs report SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY_OR_ANON: true.

site/index.html includes a browser SQL console powered by sql.js loading demo/schema.sql + demo/seed.sql for live read-only queries, a live JSON endpoint preview panel that fetches site/api/*.json, and a copy-pasteable curl block targeting the live Supabase REST URL with the publishable key.

docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md contains 10 decisions, each with named specs, conflict prose, choice prose, per-spec mapping table, and tradeoff sentence. All required areas are covered: Person (DEC-001), Class/course/context (DEC-002), Roles (DEC-003), Enrollment vs. membership (DEC-004), Results/scores (DEC-005), Standards alignment (DEC-006), Identifiers (DEC-007), Time/session (DEC-008), Content/resource (DEC-009), Tenancy (DEC-010).

standards-overlap-decisions.md ends with a machine-readable `<!-- decision-trace:start -->` JSON block that maps each `decision_id` to a list of `produces_fields` (e.g., `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person.display_name`). Every dictionary field carries a `decision_id` attribute, and scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py enforces bidirectional linkage and emits errors when a field references an unknown decision or when produces_fields point to non-existent fields.

supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql adds `tenant_id uuid not null` to every fact table, runs `enable row level security` + `force row level security`, and creates SELECT policies whose USING clause references `auth.jwt() ->> 'tenant_id'` (not `true`). supabase/policies/pg_policies.snapshot.json confirms identical predicates with rowsecurity=true and forceRowSecurity=true. tests/supabase_tenant_rls_test.py creates two real Supabase Auth users with different tenant_id app_metadata, hits the live REST URL, and asserts neither tenant can read the other's rows.

dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json defines `oauth_scope_policies` that map specific scopes (e.g., `platform.roster.users.directory:read` with `privacy_ceiling: directory`) to exact dictionary field paths. The generated openapi/generated/integration-governance-core.v0.json emits a `platformOAuth` security scheme, an `x-oauthScopePolicies` extension, and per-field `x-oauthScopes` arrays. Each field carries a `privacy_class`, satisfying the substance check (scope mapped to fields with `privacy_class: directory`, not just `roster:read`).

Every field across dictionary/*.v1.json carries a `privacy_class` value, and the seed dictionary at data/data-dictionary.seed.json enumerates a vocabulary used by the portal filter. However, several Caliper fields use ad-hoc values like `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` (e.g., caliper_entity.name, caliper_extension.extension_value) that are not part of the documented enumerated vocabulary, undermining the requirement that values be drawn from a fixed list.

docs/admin-operations.md lists one allowlist row for `tenant_rls_test_auth_fixture_setup` with caller `tests/supabase_tenant_rls_test.py`, a specific `why_user_jwt_insufficient` reason ('test must create temporary Supabase Auth users with different app_metadata.tenant_id claims before it can verify user-JWT RLS isolation'), and `last_reviewed: 2026-04-26` (within 30 days of the 2026-04-24 research date). tests/supabase_tenant_rls_test.py contains the matching inline comment 'Admin operation: tenant_rls_test_auth_fixture_setup'. No other service-role usages appear in evidence.

No `audit_log` table appears in supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql, no Postgres trigger on restricted views exists, and no Supabase Edge Function wraps restricted reads (supabase/functions/* not present in evidence). docs/spec-gap-backlog.md item 18 explicitly says 'Not done' for audit logging, and no test demonstrates an audit row written after a restricted-field read.

docs/spec-gap-backlog.md includes a 'Rubric Trace For Open Items' table that maps several rubric IDs to backlog work (lead_spec_full_accounting, tenant_isolation_enforced, oauth_scopes_mapped_to_fields, audit_log_for_sensitive_reads, edge_functions_for_non_crud_endpoints, edge_functions_propagate_user_jwt, rls_enabled_on_referenced_tables). However, items that this evaluation marks partial/fail — `privacy_class_on_every_field` and `gap_backlog_current` itself — have no rubric-id-keyed entries, so the backlog does not yet cover every unmet rubric item.

docs/dictionary-coverage-matrix.md has explicit columns for Structured source, Generated artifacts, Runnable slice, and Unsupported ledger and populates them honestly per spec (e.g., QTI/CASE/Caliper/LTI rows show 'No' under Runnable slice instead of deflecting to backlog).

No supabase/functions/ directory or any deno.json/index.ts files appear in the evidence. docs/spec-gap-backlog.md items 20–22 confirm QTI import, Caliper ingestion, LTI launch, and gradebook bulk operations are 'Docs only' with 'Not done' status. Runtime prereq report shows supabase_cli: true and SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: true, so the documented blocking conditions are not met — this is a code gap, not an external blocker.

The rubric explicitly states this item is blocked when no Edge Functions exist yet (covered by `edge_functions_for_non_crud_endpoints`). No supabase/functions/* directories appear in the evidence, so there are no functions to grade for JWT propagation.
blocked_prerequisites: At least one Supabase Edge Function must exist before JWT-propagation can be evaluated (depends on edge_functions_for_non_crud_endpoints)

items.reason: supabase/policies/pg_policies.snapshot.json shows every runtime-backed OneRoster table (organizations, people, academic_sessions, courses, classes, enrollments, line_items, results, source_identifiers) with `rowsecurity: true`, `forceRowSecurity: true`, and a non-trivial USING predicate scoping to `auth.jwt() ->> 'tenant_id'`. generatedAt is 2026-04-26T10:20:11Z, consistent with the recent migration. No `using (true)` policies appear.

scripts/check_supabase_rest.py issues real HTTPS calls against https://qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.supabase.co/rest/v1/{people,class_roster,gradebook_results} using the publishable key and asserts 200 responses. docs/supabase-hosted-database.md documents the verified status as of 2026-04-26, and runtime prereqs confirm the publishable key is present.

site/index.html includes a copy-pasteable curl block: `curl 'https://qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.supabase.co/rest/v1/people?select=id,display_name&order=id.asc&limit=1' -H 'apikey: sb_publishable_...' -H 'authorization: Bearer sb_publishable_...'`. The publishable key is the public one and no extra env vars are required.

VERIFY.md ends with `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`, and scripts/evaluate_platform.py writes a per-rubric-item array under `items` plus summary counts (`pass`, `partial`, `fail`, `blocked`, `total`) and a `done` boolean.

scripts/check_spec_conformance.py is documented as advisory-only ('ADVISORY ONLY as of the LLM-evaluator redesign... Do not add new gating logic here') and still writes site/api/spec-conformance.json. scripts/codex_loop.py gates publish via `maybe_publish` using `judge_result` and `after_eval` (LLM evaluator), and scripts/evaluate_platform.py loads the deterministic report into `advisory_block` passed to the LLM, satisfying the 'one signal among many' requirement.

scripts/codex_loop.py defines `read_overrides()` reading docs/loop-overrides.md, `overrides_paused()` that short-circuits before the iteration runs when a line equals 'PAUSE' (HTML comments stripped), and `format_overrides_for_prompt()` injected into build_prompt under a 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md` (AUTHORITATIVE...)' section. site/docs/index.html confirms loop-overrides.html exists, implying the source markdown is present.

scripts/codex_loop.py terminates on `after_eval.get('done')` returned by the LLM evaluator (not a numeric score). scripts/evaluate_platform.py computes `done = counts['fail'] == 0 and counts['partial'] == 0 and (counts['blocked'] == 0 or all_blocked_have_prereqs(verdicts))`. Hard caps remain via DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS=25 and DEFAULT_STALL_LIMIT=3.

advisoryScoreGate.note: Advisory only. The deterministic score gate no longer gates publish; this evaluator does.
